bio_short: The NIH Office of Dietary Supplements (ODS) strengthens knowledge and understanding of dietary supplements by evaluating scientific information, stimulating and supporting research, and disseminating research results.
focus_area: dietary supplement evidence synthesis and fact sheets

full_name: FDA Center for Food Safety and Applied Nutrition
bio_short: The FDA's CFSAN oversees dietary supplements under DSHEA (1994). It does not require premarket approval but monitors safety and can take action against adulterated or misbranded products.
focus_area: dietary supplement regulation and consumer safety

professional_titles: Chief, Division of Preventive Medicine, Brigham and Women's Hospital
Michael and Lee Bell Professor of Women's Health, Harvard Medical School
Professor of Epidemiology, Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health
primary_affiliations: Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health
bio_short: Principal investigator of the landmark VITAL trial (25,871 participants) testing vitamin D3 (2,000 IU/day) and omega-3 fatty acids for primary prevention of cancer and CVD. Also leads the COSMOS multivitamin/cocoa flavanol trial. One of the world's most cited researchers in preventive medicine.
focus_area: large-scale RCTs on vitamin D and omega-3 supplementation for cancer and CVD prevention

professional_titles: Professor, Endocrinology, Diabetes, Nutrition & Weight Management, BU School of Medicine
bio_short: The world's foremost vitamin D researcher. Identified the major circulating form of vitamin D (25-hydroxyvitamin D) used worldwide to measure status. First to isolate and chemically synthesize active vitamin D. Author of 400+ publications on vitamin D biochemistry, physiology, and photobiology. Author of The Vitamin D Solution (2010).
focus_area: vitamin D photobiology, optimal serum levels, and supplementation protocols

professional_titles: Senior Advisor, Cleveland Clinic Center for Functional Medicine
primary_affiliations: Cleveland Clinic Center for Functional Medicine
bio_short: Leading functional medicine physician and prolific health author. Advocates a 'food as medicine' approach with strategic supplementation. Recommends a 'Foundational Five' supplement stack (multivitamin, omega-3, vitamin D3, magnesium glycinate, probiotic).
focus_area: functional medicine approach to supplementation and chronic disease prevention

professional_titles: Fredrick John Stare Professor of Epidemiology and Nutrition (Emeritus), Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health
Professor of Medicine, Harvard Medical School
primary_affiliations: Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health
bio_short: One of the most cited nutrition scientists in the world. Ran the Nurses' Health Study and has contributed foundational research on diet and chronic disease. Considers daily multivitamin use a reasonable precaution for most adults as a form of nutritional insurance.
focus_area: nutritional epidemiology, dietary patterns, and preventive nutrition

professional_titles: Professor of Neurobiology and Ophthalmology, Stanford School of Medicine
bio_short: Neuroscientist and public educator best known for the Huberman Lab podcast, consistently ranked #1 in the health category worldwide. Recommends evidence-informed supplement protocols including omega-3, magnesium L-threonate, and vitamin D3.
focus_area: neuroscience-informed health optimization and supplementation protocols

bio_short: Biomedical scientist with a PhD from St. Jude/University of Tennessee. One of the most prominent science communicators on micronutrients, mitochondria, and longevity. Advocates lab-guided, high-dose omega-3 and vitamin D supplementation based on published RCT evidence.
focus_area: micronutrient science, omega-3 index optimization, vitamin D, and longevity biology

primary_affiliations: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
bio_short: Independent expert panel that makes evidence-based recommendations about clinical preventive services including screening, counseling, and preventive medications for adults and children. USPSTF grades reflect certainty and magnitude of net benefit.
focus_area: preventive medicine clinical guidelines including vitamin D, calcium, and folic acid supplementation

bio_short: The AAP is the leading professional organization for pediatricians. It recommends 400 IU/day vitamin D for all breastfed infants starting soon after birth, and 1 mg/kg/day iron for exclusively breastfed infants from 4 months.
focus_area: pediatric nutrition recommendations including vitamin D and iron supplementation for infants

full_name: American College of Obstetricians and Gynecologists
bio_short: ACOG is the leading US professional society for ob-gyns. It recommends prenatal vitamins with folic acid and iron for all pregnant women, advocates folic acid 400 mcg pre-conceptionally to prevent NTDs, and endorses low-dose iron supplementation starting first trimester.
focus_area: prenatal and perinatal nutrition including folic acid, iron, and calcium supplementation during pregnancy

bio_short: The AHA is the leading US cardiovascular health organization. It recommends ~1 g/day EPA+DHA for patients with documented coronary heart disease, and 4 g/day prescription omega-3s for hypertriglyceridemia. Does not make routine omega-3 supplement recommendations for healthy adults.
focus_area: cardiovascular disease prevention including omega-3 supplementation guidelines

bio_short: The Endocrine Society issued a landmark 2024 Clinical Practice Guideline on vitamin D supplementation for disease prevention that replaces its 2011 guideline. It recommends against empiric supplementation for healthy adults under 75, but supports supplementation for children 1-18, adults 75+, pregnant women, and high-risk pre-diabetics.
focus_area: endocrine system health, hormone optimization, and vitamin D guidelines

bio_short: The WHO provides global nutrition guidelines including calcium supplementation in populations with low dietary intake during pregnancy (1.5-2.0 g/day), folic acid for all women of childbearing age (400 mcg/day), and zinc supplementation for childhood diarrhea in resource-limited settings.
focus_area: global public health nutrition guidelines for maternal, infant, and child populations

bio_short: A nonprofit public health organization that promotes optimal vitamin D levels through citizen science research. Advocates maintaining serum 25(OH)D at 40-60 ng/mL via supplementation of 1,500-2,000 IU/day or more, based on a panel of leading vitamin D scientists worldwide.
focus_area: vitamin D citizen science, serum level optimization, and expert consensus on higher-end supplementation

bio_short: A nonprofit, standards-setting organization that establishes quality, purity, identity, and strength standards for dietary supplements. The USP Verified Mark is widely recognized as the #1 recommended quality seal by healthcare professionals for dietary supplements.
focus_area: dietary supplement quality standards and third-party verification

bio_short: An independent public health and safety organization that certifies dietary supplements to NSF/ANSI 173, the only American National Standard for testing dietary supplements. Also offers NSF Certified for Sport program for athletes.
focus_area: dietary supplement and food safety certification and testing

bio_short: Pharmacy industry professional who writes on supplement quality and the pharmacist's role in guiding patients toward third-party verified products. Author of Pharmacy Times' guide to quality supplements emphasizing the USP Verified Mark.
focus_area: pharmacist guidance on supplement quality and third-party verification

bio_short: Industry expert cited in Pharmacy Times as advocating for 'truth, transparency, and trust' in the supplement industry, emphasizing the importance of third-party verification programs for consumer safety.
focus_area: supplement quality education and consumer protection in the supplement industry

professional_titles: Medical Publishing Division of Harvard Medical School
bio_short: Harvard Health Publishing is the consumer health information division of Harvard Medical School. It produces evidence-based health content including the supplements scorecard, which provides a critical, research-grounded review of popular supplements.
focus_area: consumer health education on supplements, chronic disease, and preventive nutrition

full_name: CDC Division of Nutrition, Physical Activity, and Obesity
primary_affiliations: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
bio_short: The CDC's nutrition division promotes public health nutrition including folate/folic acid supplementation to prevent neural tube defects and iron supplementation for at-risk populations. US food fortification with folic acid since 1998 has prevented approximately 1,300 NTD-affected births annually.
focus_area: public health nutrition, folic acid fortification, and NTD prevention

bio_short: One of the top-ranked academic medical centers in the US. Mayo Clinic's patient education content on vitamin D covers RDA recommendations (600-800 IU/day), safe supplementation range (1,000-2,000 IU/day), and risks of excess intake (>4,000 IU/day).
focus_area: evidence-based patient education on vitamins, minerals, and supplement safety

bio_short: Top-ranked U.S. academic medical center known for evidence-based patient education. Its health essentials content on omega-3 fatty acids, including nuanced perspectives on OTC fish oil vs. prescription omega-3s and the importance of food-first approaches, is widely accessed by consumers.
focus_area: cardiology, nutrition, and patient education on omega-3 fatty acids and heart health

bio_short: A USDA/NIH-managed portal that aggregates government-vetted nutrition information including dietary supplement guidance. Serves as a consumer-facing entry point to NIH ODS fact sheets and other federal nutrition resources.
focus_area: curated government nutrition information including dietary supplement resources

bio_short: Registered Dietitian Nutritionist at Cleveland Clinic. Cited in Cleveland Clinic's evidence-based explainer on omega-3 fatty acids, emphasizing the importance of food-first sources of EPA and DHA and recommending fish consumption over supplements for most adults.
focus_area: cardiac nutrition, omega-3 sources, and dietary supplement guidance for heart health

primary_affiliations: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
bio_short: The HHS Office on Women's Health provides comprehensive health information for women including nutrition during pregnancy, folic acid recommendations for NTD prevention, and iron supplementation guidance.
focus_area: women's health nutrition including folic acid, iron, and prenatal vitamins

bio_short: An independent, evidence-based resource that summarizes human clinical research on supplements and nutrition. Provides evidence grades, mechanism summaries, and population-specific notes for hundreds of nutrients and supplements. Not affiliated with supplement manufacturers.
focus_area: evidence grading and summarization of human clinical research on dietary supplements

professional_titles: Chief, Calcium and Bone Section, Division of Endocrinology, Brigham and Women's Hospital
Professor of Medicine, Harvard Medical School
bio_short: Co-investigator on the VITAL trial ancillary study on bone health. Expert on calcium and vitamin D for fracture prevention and osteoporosis management. Works alongside JoAnn Manson at BWH on large-scale prevention trials.
focus_area: bone health, calcium/vitamin D supplementation, and fracture prevention

bio_short: A leading professional publication for pharmacists providing continuing education, clinical guidance, and patient education resources on dietary supplements, including guidance on third-party verification and pharmacist consultation.
focus_area: pharmacist education and clinical guidance on dietary supplement quality and patient counseling

bio_short: Harvard endocrinologist who contributed to the Harvard Health supplements scorecard. Advises patients to estimate dietary calcium intake before supplementing to avoid excess, citing cardiovascular risk signals from high-dose calcium supplements.
focus_area: calcium management, bone health, and osteoporosis prevention

bio_short: A team of researchers who produce unbiased, systematic summaries of clinical evidence on dietary supplements. Known for their evidence-based ratings and independence from commercial supplement interests.
focus_area: systematic supplement evidence review and evidence-grade scoring

primary_affiliations: American College of Obstetricians and Gynecologists
bio_short: The ACOG committee responsible for producing clinical guidance including committee opinions on nutrition during pregnancy. Recommends folic acid 400-800 mcg/day pre-conceptionally, iron 27 mg/day during pregnancy, and prenatal vitamins for all pregnant women.
focus_area: clinical guidance on nutrition and supplementation during pregnancy and the perinatal period

bio_short: The official news outlet of Harvard University, the Harvard Gazette publishes accessible research summaries and expert interviews including JoAnn Manson's VITAL trial results and omega-3 cardiovascular findings.
focus_area: science journalism covering Harvard research including nutrition, supplements, and preventive medicine

full_name: WHO Department of Nutrition and Food Safety
bio_short: The WHO's technical arm responsible for producing evidence-based nutrition guidelines including calcium supplementation in pregnancy for pre-eclampsia prevention and folic acid for all women of childbearing age.
focus_area: global maternal, infant, and child nutrition guidelines and supplementation recommendations

full_name: CDC National Center on Birth Defects and Developmental Disabilities
primary_affiliations: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
bio_short: CDC center responsible for NTD surveillance and folic acid public health campaigns. Tracks the impact of mandatory folic acid fortification (1998), which has prevented approximately 1,300 NTD-affected births annually in the US.
focus_area: NTD prevention, folic acid supplementation and fortification, and birth defect surveillance
